title: Web Development - 50 Words
definition: 50 essential terms for building and understanding websites and web applications.

words.meaning: The standard markup language used to structure content on the web.
words.sentence: This HTML file defines the structure of the homepage.

words.meaning: A stylesheet language used to describe the visual presentation of a webpage.
words.sentence: We used CSS to make the buttons rounded and blue.

words.meaning: A programming language used to add interactivity to web pages.
words.sentence: JavaScript handles the form validation on this page.

words.meaning: Document Object Model - a programming interface representing a webpage's structure.
words.sentence: The script modifies the DOM to add a new element.

words.meaning: A design approach ensuring a website works well on all screen sizes.
words.sentence: Thanks to responsive design, the site looks great on mobile too.

words.meaning: The part of an application users interact with directly.
words.sentence: The frontend team redesigned the checkout page.

words.meaning: The server-side part of an application handling logic and data.
words.sentence: The backend processes the payment and updates the database.

words.meaning: Involving both frontend and backend development.
words.sentence: She's a full stack developer, comfortable with both React and Node.

words.meaning: Software used to access and display websites.
words.sentence: The site looks different in this browser due to a rendering bug.

words.meaning: The process of generating a visual display from code.
words.sentence: Rendering the page takes longer with this many images.

words.meaning: A screen width at which a design's layout changes.
words.sentence: The menu collapses into a hamburger icon at this breakpoint.

words.meaning: A web app that loads a single HTML page and updates content dynamically.
words.sentence: This single-page application never fully reloads the browser.

words.meaning: Generating a webpage's HTML on the server before sending it to the browser.
words.sentence: Server-side rendering improves initial load performance.

words.meaning: Referring to code or processing that happens in the user's browser.
words.sentence: This validation happens client-side before the form is submitted.

words.meaning: A reusable, encapsulated custom HTML element.
words.sentence: We built a web component for the reusable navigation bar.

words.meaning: A pattern used to select and style HTML elements.
words.sentence: This CSS selector targets all buttons with the 'primary' class.

words.meaning: A CSS layout model for arranging elements in rows or columns.
words.sentence: We used flexbox to align the icons evenly across the row.

words.meaning: A CSS system for arranging elements into rows and columns.
words.sentence: The dashboard uses a grid layout for its widgets.

words.meaning: A CSS technique to apply styles based on device characteristics.
words.sentence: This media query hides the sidebar on small screens.

words.meaning: The visible area of a web page on a device screen.
words.sentence: The viewport meta tag ensures proper scaling on mobile.

words.meaning: A clickable reference that takes users to another page or section.
words.sentence: Click the hyperlink to go to the pricing page.

words.meaning: A small piece of data stored by a browser to remember information.
words.sentence: The cookie stores the user's login session.

words.meaning: A browser feature that stores data persistently on the client's device.
words.sentence: We save the user's theme preference in local storage.

words.meaning: Browser storage that persists only for the duration of a page session.
words.sentence: The form draft is saved in session storage while typing.

words.meaning: Cross-Origin Resource Sharing - a security feature controlling requests between different domains.
words.sentence: We got a CORS error trying to call the API from a different domain.

words.meaning: Designing websites usable by people with disabilities.
words.sentence: We added alt text to images to improve web accessibility.

words.meaning: HTML that describes the meaning of content, not just its appearance.
words.sentence: Using semantic HTML tags like <article> improves accessibility and SEO.

words.meaning: A web application that offers app-like features such as offline use.
words.sentence: Our progressive web app works offline and can be installed on phones.

words.meaning: A tool that combines multiple code files into optimized bundles.
words.sentence: The bundler combines all our JavaScript files into one file.

words.meaning: A tool that converts code from one language version to another.
words.sentence: The transpiler converts modern JavaScript into a version older browsers support.

words.meaning: The process of removing unnecessary characters from code to reduce file size.
words.sentence: Minification reduced our CSS file size by 40%.

words.meaning: Loading content only when it's needed, to improve performance.
words.sentence: Images below the fold use lazy loading to speed up the page.

words.meaning: A protocol enabling real-time, two-way communication between browser and server.
words.sentence: We use a web socket to push live chat messages instantly.

words.meaning: A technique for sending and receiving data without reloading the page.
words.sentence: AJAX fetches new comments without refreshing the whole page.

words.meaning: A network of servers that deliver web content quickly based on location.
words.sentence: We use a Content Delivery Network to serve images faster worldwide.

words.meaning: Search Engine Optimization - practices to improve a site's visibility in search results.
words.sentence: Good SEO helped our blog rank higher on Google.

words.meaning: An HTML element providing metadata about a webpage.
words.sentence: The meta tag sets the page description shown in search results.

words.meaning: The small icon representing a website in browser tabs.
words.sentence: Don't forget to add a favicon before launch.

words.meaning: A service that stores website files and makes them accessible online.
words.sentence: We chose a reliable web hosting provider for the launch.

words.meaning: The human-readable address used to access a website.
words.sentence: We registered a new domain name for the product launch.

words.meaning: Domain Name System - translates domain names into IP addresses.
words.sentence: DNS propagation can take up to 48 hours after a change.

words.meaning: A digital certificate that enables secure, encrypted connections to a website.
words.sentence: We installed an SSL certificate to enable HTTPS.

words.meaning: A secure version of HTTP that encrypts data between browser and server.
words.sentence: All pages on our site now load over HTTPS.

words.meaning: A program that automatically browses the web to index content.
words.sentence: Search engine web crawlers index new pages within a few days.

words.meaning: A website made of fixed files that don't change per request.
words.sentence: Our documentation is hosted as a static site for speed.

words.meaning: Web content that changes based on user input or data.
words.sentence: The dashboard shows dynamic content based on your account.

words.meaning: A tool that generates HTML by combining templates with data.
words.sentence: The template engine inserts the user's name into the greeting.

words.meaning: Guidelines defining how to make web content accessible to people with disabilities.
words.sentence: We audited the site against the WCAG accessibility standard.

words.meaning: Code that adds modern functionality to older browsers that lack native support.
words.sentence: We added a polyfill so older browsers support this new feature.

words.meaning: An HTML structure that collects user input on a webpage.
words.sentence: The web form validates the email address before submission.

quiz.question: What does DOM stand for?
quiz.explanation: The DOM represents a webpage's structure as a programmable object tree.

quiz.question: What is 'lazy loading'?
quiz.explanation: Lazy loading improves performance by deferring loading until necessary.

quiz.question: What does CORS control?
quiz.explanation: CORS is a security feature governing cross-origin requests.

quiz.question: ___ is the standard markup language used to structure web content.
quiz.explanation: HTML (HyperText Markup Language) structures webpage content.

quiz.question: What is a 'Progressive Web App'?
options: A web app with app-like features such as offline use
quiz.answer: A web app with app-like features such as offline use
quiz.explanation: PWAs offer native app-like experiences within a browser.
